parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf09084-order`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Pull Side A (Google Sheets)

### Pull Side A (Google Sheets)

Reads every row from 'Order Tracker' — the read side of the store, e.g. for a digest or a downstream sync.

Ports: ①→ Diff the Two Systems · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Pull Side B (Airtable)

Searches Airtable's 'Orders' table by formula (`={LastModified} > DATEADD(NOW(), -1, 'days')`) for records the pipeline hasn't handled yet.

Ports: ①→ Diff the Two Systems · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Diff the Two Systems

Diffs two streams by key into 4 lanes: only-in-A, same, different, only-in-B — the heart of the sync.

Ports: ①→ Create Missing in Airtable · ②→ Count In-Sync Rows · ③→ Apply Updates to Airtable · ④→ Backfill into Google Sheets

### Create Missing in Airtable

Creates a new record in Airtable's 'Orders' table.

Ports: ①→ Gather Sync Results · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Count In-Sync Rows

Collapses all items into a single summary item (for counts and run stats).

Next: Gather Sync Results

### Apply Updates to Airtable

Upserts into Airtable's 'Orders' table, matched on `orderId` — safe to re-run without creating duplicates.

Ports: ①→ Gather Sync Results · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Backfill into Google Sheets

Appends a new row to 'Order Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Gather Sync Results · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Gather Sync Results

Rejoins 4 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Cap Batch Volume

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 100 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Tag Pipeline Metadata

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Sort Orders Newest First

### Sort Orders Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Order Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Outlook · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Outlook

Sends an email via Microsoft Outlook — recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Outlook

### Escalate Failure to Outlook

Sends an email via Microsoft Outlook — recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

# Process new orders with Shopify and Google Drive hands-free

**Pattern:** Order Fulfillment · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Process new orders with Shopify and Google Drive hands-free. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual inventory is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Pull Side A (Google Sheets) → Diff the Two Systems → Create Missing in Airtable → Gather Sync Results → Cap Batch Volume → Align Field Names → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to order fulfillment without manual effort
- Anyone automating inventory in e-commerce
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Scales without extra headcount
- Standardizes the process every time
- Runs 24/7 in the background

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Airtable PAT; Google OAuth2 / service account; Microsoft OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Compare Datasets does the matching instead of hand-rolled join logic — one node produces four labeled lanes (only-in-A, matching, different, only-in-B), and each lane gets exactly the action it needs (create / count / update / backfill) instead of one generic 'sync' branch trying to cover every case.

## Practice exercises

You've got the basics covered — push further:

1. Swap Side A or Side B for a third platform and rewire the merge-by-fields key.
2. Intentionally create a mismatch between the two systems and trace which lane it lands in.
3. Change mergeByFields to a different key and see how the diff results shift.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your inventory process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
